metadata.title: DagsHub + RunPod: Full Infrastructure Migration Plan
metadata.estimated_cost: ~$2 for smoke test, ~$5-6 for debug factorial pass

expert.assessment: DagsHub provides a free individual tier with 20 GB storage, unlimited public repo experiments, and an MLflow server at https://dagshub.com/USER/REPO.mlflow. The DVC remote uses S3-compatible API at https://dagshub.com/USER/REPO.s3. GitHub mirror sync via webhooks (auto-sync on push, 24h periodic). Key limitation: GitHub connection requires web UI (no programmatic API). dagshub.init() is the recommended Python integration — sets MLflow URI + auth in one call. Auth via MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME + MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD env vars (token works as both username and password).
expert.recommendation: Use DagsHub as primary MLflow tracking server (zero infra cost). Use DagsHub storage as DVC remote for MiniVess + DeepVess (~5 GB, well within 20 GB). Do NOT store SAM3/VesselFM checkpoints on DagsHub (900 MB per run eats storage fast). Connect GitHub mirror via web UI (one-time manual step, then auto-syncs).

expert.assessment: RunPod container-native execution with SkyPilot is proven (11 passes of experience). RTX 4090 at $0.34/hr community cloud is the cost-efficiency winner. Network Volume at /opt/vol persists data, weights, and mlruns across jobs. Key change: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI shifts from /opt/vol/mlruns (file-based) to https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow (remote server). This ELIMINATES the mandatory sky rsync down step for MLflow data. Model weights still cached on Network Volume (SAM3 9 GB, VesselFM 2 GB). DVC data can come from DagsHub storage OR Network Volume (both valid).
expert.recommendation: Update smoke_test_gpu.yaml and train_factorial.yaml: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to DagsHub. Add MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME and MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD to SkyPilot envs. Keep Network Volume for weight caching (saves 15-20 min per job setup). DVC pull from DagsHub storage in setup block (replaces GCS DVC pull). Data already on Network Volume should be detected and reused (existing logic works).

expert.assessment: End-to-end flow: Laptop -> SkyPilot -> RunPod RTX 4090 -> DagsHub MLflow. Three integration points: (1) MLflow tracking URI, (2) DVC remote, (3) Git mirror. MLflow artifacts stored on DagsHub (mlflow-artifacts:/ scheme, auto-configured). DVC data stored on DagsHub S3-compatible endpoint. No Cloud SQL, no Cloud Run, no Pulumi, no GCS buckets needed. The resolve_tracking_uri() function in src/minivess must handle DagsHub URLs. Docker image still pulled from Docker Hub (petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest). Prefect remains local orchestration (PREFECT_DISABLED=1 on RunPod, as before).
expert.recommendation: The architecture simplifies dramatically. Three components: (1) Docker Hub (image registry) -- already working (2) RunPod (compute) -- already working (3) DagsHub (tracking + data) -- NEW, replaces GCS + Cloud Run MLflow + Cloud SQL Update .env.example with DAGSHUB_TOKEN, DAGSHUB_REPO_OWNER, DAGSHUB_REPO_NAME. Update resolve_tracking_uri() to validate DagsHub MLflow URLs. MLflow artifact size awareness: log only metrics/params to DagsHub, keep large checkpoint files on Network Volume or use DagsHub Connect GCS for overflow.

expert.assessment: GCP was costing $53-131/month in always-on infrastructure (Cloud SQL, Cloud Run, SkyPilot controller, GAR storage, GCS). Now torn down = $0/month. DagsHub free tier: $0/month (public repo, unlimited experiments). RunPod Network Volume 25 GB: $1.75/month. RunPod RTX 4090 compute: $0.34/hr, ~$4.21 per 34-job debug pass. Total debug pass cost: ~$4.21 compute + $1.75 storage = ~$5.96/pass. 20 GB DagsHub storage: MiniVess (~1.5 GB) + DeepVess (~0.5 GB) = ~2 GB DVC data. MLflow artifacts (metrics/params only, no checkpoints): ~50 MB per pass. Headroom: ~17 GB free for future datasets, experiments. Break-even vs GCP: DagsHub+RunPod saves $50-130/month MINIMUM.
expert.recommendation: DagsHub free tier is sufficient for this project's lifetime. Do NOT log large artifacts (checkpoints, model files) to DagsHub MLflow -- they eat the 20 GB fast. Log metrics, params, tags only. Keep checkpoints on Network Volume and/or local filesystem. If 20 GB becomes tight, connect external GCS bucket ($0.02/GB/month) via DagsHub Connect External Storage feature. RTX 4090 remains the cost-efficiency champion. No change needed.

expert.assessment: For Nature Protocols, a reviewer needs to reproduce results from scratch. New setup steps: (1) Create RunPod account, (2) Create DagsHub account, (3) Clone repo, (4) sky check runpod, (5) sky jobs launch. DagsHub MLflow means reviewer can VIEW experiment results in browser without running anything (public repo = public MLflow UI). DagsHub DVC means reviewer can pull data with standard DVC commands. No GCP project, no Pulumi, no Cloud SQL, no IAM service accounts. Consumer GPU path: RTX 4090 at $0.34/hr, credit card only. Local GPU path: RTX 2070+ with 8 GB VRAM for DynUNet, local MLflow. The file-based MLflow fallback (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=mlruns) still works for fully offline/local execution.
expert.recommendation: Document three tiers in the paper: Tier 0 ($0): Local GPU, local MLflow, local data. Zero external dependencies. Tier 1 (~$5/pass): RunPod RTX 4090, DagsHub MLflow, DagsHub DVC. Tier 2 (~$50/month): Full managed stack (GCP, for labs that want it -- documented but not the default path). The DagsHub public MLflow UI is a huge win for open science -- reviewers and readers can browse all experiments without running a single command. Include DagsHub experiment URLs in the paper supplementary materials.

round.debate:
E1 and E4 debated artifact storage strategy. E4 warned that logging checkpoints to DagsHub MLflow would exhaust 20 GB in ~20 runs. E1 confirmed that DagsHub auto-configures mlflow-artifacts:/ location, but large files count against storage. RESOLUTION: Log metrics/params/tags to DagsHub MLflow. Keep checkpoints on Network Volume. If a researcher wants checkpoint sharing, use DagsHub Connect with external GCS bucket (pay-as-you-go).
E2 and E3 debated DVC data source on RunPod. E2 preferred Network Volume cache (already uploaded, zero latency). E3 preferred DagsHub DVC pull (reproducible, no manual upload step). RESOLUTION: Dual path. Check Network Volume first (fast path). If data not present, DVC pull from DagsHub remote (reproducible path). This is the same pattern as the current smoke_test_gpu.yaml but with DagsHub instead of GCS.
E5 raised concern about DagsHub being a smaller company -- longevity risk. E3 countered that all DagsHub data is standard MLflow + DVC, fully exportable. E1 confirmed DagsHub repos can be exported via git clone + dvc pull at any time. RESOLUTION: Acceptable risk. File-based MLflow remains the offline fallback. DagsHub is an OVERLAY, not a hard dependency.

round.consensus: All 5 experts converge on: DagsHub as primary MLflow + DVC backend, RunPod as primary compute, with file-based MLflow as offline fallback. The architecture is: Laptop (control plane) -> SkyPilot -> RunPod RTX 4090 -> DagsHub MLflow. DVC data on DagsHub storage (primary) + Network Volume cache (fast path). Model weights on Network Volume only (not on DagsHub, too large). GitHub mirror via web UI (one-time manual step). Total monthly cost: ~$1.75 (Network Volume) + compute-on-demand. Zero always-on infrastructure. Pay only for what you compute.

architecture.diagram: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ DagsHub (free individual tier, public repo) │ │ https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia │ │ ├── MLflow Tracking Server (.mlflow endpoint) │ │ │ └── Experiment metrics, params, tags (unlimited for public) │ │ ├── DVC Storage (.s3 endpoint, 20 GB) │ │ │ └── MiniVess + DeepVess data (~2 GB) │ │ ├── Git Mirror (webhook sync from GitHub) │ │ └── MLflow Artifacts (mlflow-artifacts:/ scheme) │ │ └── Small artifacts only (plots, configs) — NO checkpoints │ └────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ HTTPS (MLflow tracking + DVC pull) │ ┌────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Researcher's Laptop │ (Control Plane) │ │ ├── SkyPilot CLI ─────────┼──→ RunPod API │ │ ├── mlruns/ (local backup)│ │ │ ├── DVC data/ (local) │ │ │ └── Docker images (build) │ │ └────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ SkyPilot SSH + Docker image pull │ ┌────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ RunPod RTX 4090 ($0.34/hr)│ (Compute) │ │ ├── Docker: petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest │ │ ├── MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=https://dagshub.com/.../vascadia.mlflow │ │ └── Network Volume (/opt/vol/) │ │ ├── weights/ (SAM3 9 GB, VesselFM 2 GB) — cached forever │ │ ├── data/ (MiniVess ~1.5 GB) — cache, DVC pull fallback │ │ └── checkpoints/ (training outputs, local persistence) │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

phase.name: DagsHub Configuration
phase.description: Configure DagsHub as the MLflow tracking server and DVC remote. The DagsHub repo already exists at https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia. This phase makes it functional: MLflow endpoint, DVC storage, GitHub mirror.

task.name: Install dagshub Python SDK
task.description: Add dagshub client library as a project dependency.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
# Add dagshub as a dependency (agents extra group — lightweight integration tooling) uv add dagshub
# Verify installation uv run python -c "import dagshub; print(f'dagshub version: {dagshub.__version__}')"
task.verification: dagshub imports successfully, version printed.

task.name: Authenticate with DagsHub
task.description: Login to DagsHub CLI to cache credentials locally. This generates a token stored in ~/.dagshub/token.
task.commands:
# Option A: Interactive login (opens browser) uv run dagshub login
# Option B: If you already have a token, set it directly # export DAGSHUB_TOKEN=your_token_here # uv run python -c " # import dagshub.auth # dagshub.auth.add_app_token(token='your_token_here') # "
# Verify authentication uv run python -c " import dagshub dagshub.init(repo_owner='petteriTeikari', repo_name='vascadia') print('DagsHub authentication OK') "
task.verification: dagshub.init() succeeds without authentication errors.
task.notes: The dagshub login command opens a browser for OAuth. After login, a token is cached at ~/.dagshub/token. This token is used for MLflow auth and DVC auth. For CI/cloud environments, use DAGSHUB_TOKEN env var instead.

task.name: Connect GitHub mirror via DagsHub web UI
task.description: Connect the existing GitHub repo (github.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia) to the DagsHub repo. This enables automatic webhook-based sync on every push. NOTE: This step requires the DagsHub web UI — no programmatic API exists.
task.commands: # This is a MANUAL step via the DagsHub web UI: # 1. Go to https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia # 2. If the repo was created as "Connect A Repo" from GitHub, sync is already active. # 3. If created as empty repo: # a. Click "Settings" (gear icon) # b. Look for "Git Integration" or "Connected Services" # c. Click "Connect GitHub" → Authorize → Select petteriTeikari/vascadia # 4. Verify: Push to GitHub, check DagsHub shows the commit within minutes. # # Sync frequency: Webhook-based (instant on push) + 24h periodic backup sync. # Manual sync: Click sync button next to project name on DagsHub.
task.verification: Push a test commit to GitHub, verify it appears on DagsHub within 5 minutes. Check: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia/commits/
task.notes: DagsHub GitHub integration uses webhooks. Once connected, PRs created on GitHub appear on DagsHub and vice versa. Issues sync bidirectionally. The connection is one-time; subsequent syncs are automatic.

task.name: Configure DagsHub as DVC remote
task.description: Add DagsHub S3-compatible storage as a DVC remote. DagsHub provides an S3-compatible endpoint at https://dagshub.com/USER/REPO.s3 for each repo. Authentication uses the DagsHub token as both access_key_id and secret_access_key.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
# Add DagsHub as a DVC remote (S3-compatible endpoint) dvc remote add dagshub s3://dvc dvc remote modify dagshub endpointurl https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3
# Set authentication LOCALLY (--local flag keeps creds out of git) # Use your DagsHub token for both access_key_id and secret_access_key dvc remote modify dagshub --local access_key_id YOUR_DAGSHUB_TOKEN dvc remote modify dagshub --local secret_access_key YOUR_DAGSHUB_TOKEN
# Set dagshub as the default remote for this project dvc remote default dagshub
# Verify configuration (public part — no secrets) cat .dvc/config
# Verify local config has credentials (not committed to git) cat .dvc/config.local
task.verification: .dvc/config shows dagshub remote with endpointurl. .dvc/config.local shows access_key_id and secret_access_key. dvc remote default returns "dagshub".
task.notes: IMPORTANT: The --local flag stores credentials in .dvc/config.local which is gitignored by default. NEVER put tokens in .dvc/config (tracked by git). The S3 bucket name is literally "dvc" — DagsHub maps it to the repo's storage. Endpoint URL format: https://dagshub.com/OWNER/REPO.s3

task.name: Configure MLflow tracking URI for DagsHub
task.description: Set MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to the DagsHub MLflow endpoint. This is the central change that routes all experiment tracking to DagsHub. Auth via MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME + MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD env vars.
task.commands:
# Test MLflow connectivity from local machine export MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI="https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow" export MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME="petteriTeikari" export MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD="YOUR_DAGSHUB_TOKEN"
# Verify connection uv run python -c " import mlflow import os
# Set tracking URI mlflow.set_tracking_uri(os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI'])
# List experiments (should succeed if auth is correct) client = mlflow.MlflowClient() experiments = client.search_experiments(max_results=5) print(f'MLflow connected to DagsHub: {len(experiments)} experiments found') for exp in experiments: print(f' - {exp.name} (id={exp.experiment_id})') "
task.verification: MLflow client connects to DagsHub and lists experiments (at minimum the Default experiment).
task.notes: DagsHub MLflow URI format: https://dagshub.com/OWNER/REPO.mlflow Auth: MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME (DagsHub username) + MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD (token). Alternative: dagshub.init() sets these automatically, but env vars are more portable for SkyPilot YAML (where Python code doesn't run during env setup).

task.name: Test MLflow logging end-to-end
task.description: Create a test MLflow run on DagsHub to verify full write path: log_param, log_metric, log_artifact (small file).
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
uv run python -c " import dagshub import mlflow import tempfile import os
# Initialize DagsHub (auto-configures MLflow URI + auth) dagshub.init(repo_owner='petteriTeikari', repo_name='vascadia')
# Create a test experiment mlflow.set_experiment('dagshub-connectivity-test')
with mlflow.start_run(run_name='connectivity-test-001'): # Log parameters mlflow.log_param('test_param', 'dagshub_migration') mlflow.log_param('model', 'dynunet')
# Log metrics mlflow.log_metric('test_loss', 0.42) mlflow.log_metric('test_dice', 0.85)
# Log a small artifact (text file) with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.txt', delete=False) as f: f.write('DagsHub connectivity test artifact\n') f.write('If you can read this, artifact upload works.\n') tmp_path = f.name mlflow.log_artifact(tmp_path, artifact_path='test') os.unlink(tmp_path)
run = mlflow.active_run() print(f'Run ID: {run.info.run_id}') print(f'Experiment: dagshub-connectivity-test') print(f'Artifact URI: {run.info.artifact_uri}') print('SUCCESS: MLflow logging to DagsHub works!') print(f'View at: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia/experiments') "
task.verification: Run completes without errors. Visit https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia/experiments to see the test run. Verify: params (test_param, model), metrics (test_loss, test_dice), artifact (test.txt).
task.rollback: Delete the test experiment via MLflow UI on DagsHub if needed. This is a non-destructive test — no permanent changes.

phase.name: Environment Variable Updates
phase.description: Update .env.example (single source of truth) and .env (local secrets) with DagsHub configuration. This phase follows CLAUDE.md Rule #22: all configurable values MUST be in .env.example FIRST.

task.name: Update .env.example with DagsHub variables
task.description: Add DagsHub-specific environment variables to .env.example. Update MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI default to DagsHub. Update cloud provider documentation comments.
task.changes:
# In .env.example, ADD a new DagsHub section and UPDATE the MLflow section:
# ─── DagsHub (MLflow + DVC hosting — free for public repos) ────────────── # DagsHub provides a hosted MLflow server and DVC storage for every repository. # Free individual tier: 20 GB storage, unlimited experiments for public repos. # Auth: your DagsHub token works for both MLflow and DVC authentication. # # WHO NEEDS THIS: Everyone (default tracking + data backend since 2026-03-28). # WHO CAN SKIP: Anyone using local-only MLflow (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=mlruns). # # Get your token: https://dagshub.com/user/settings/tokens # Docs: https://dagshub.com/docs/integration_guide/mlflow_tracking/ DAGSHUB_TOKEN= DAGSHUB_REPO_OWNER=petteriTeikari DAGSHUB_REPO_NAME=vascadia
# UPDATE the existing MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI line: # Old: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://localhost:5000 # New: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow
# UPDATE MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME/PASSWORD comments: # DagsHub auth: username = DagsHub username, password = DagsHub token. # These env vars are read by MLflow client automatically (no code changes). MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME=petteriTeikari MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD= # Set to your DAGSHUB_TOKEN value
# UPDATE SKYPILOT_DEFAULT_CLOUD: # Old: SKYPILOT_DEFAULT_CLOUD=gcp # New: SKYPILOT_DEFAULT_CLOUD=runpod
# UPDATE DVC_REMOTE: # Old: DVC_REMOTE=upcloud # New: DVC_REMOTE=dagshub
task.verification: grep -c "DAGSHUB" .env.example returns >= 3 lines. grep "MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI" .env.example shows DagsHub URL. grep "SKYPILOT_DEFAULT_CLOUD" .env.example shows runpod.
task.notes: CLAUDE.md Rule #22: .env.example is the single source of truth. The GCP section in .env.example should be marked as OPTIONAL/ARCHIVED but NOT removed (some researchers may still use GCP). The UpCloud/Hetzner/Oracle sections should be marked ARCHIVED.

task.name: Update .env with actual credentials
task.description: Update the local .env file with actual DagsHub token and credentials. This file is gitignored — never committed.
task.commands:
# .env is gitignored — edit manually or via script # The user should: # 1. Get their DagsHub token from https://dagshub.com/user/settings/tokens # 2. Set in .env: # DAGSHUB_TOKEN=your_actual_token # MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow # MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME=petteriTeikari # MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD=your_actual_token (same as DAGSHUB_TOKEN) # SKYPILOT_DEFAULT_CLOUD=runpod # DVC_REMOTE=dagshub
# Verify .env has the right values (redacted) grep "DAGSHUB_TOKEN" .env | head -1 | cut -c1-20 grep "MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI" .env | head -1
task.verification: .env contains non-empty DAGSHUB_TOKEN and DagsHub MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI.

task.name: Update resolve_tracking_uri() for DagsHub URLs
task.description: The resolve_tracking_uri() function validates MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI. It must accept DagsHub HTTPS URLs (https://dagshub.com/...) in addition to existing patterns (localhost, file paths, Cloud Run URLs).
task.commands:
# Find the resolve_tracking_uri function grep -rn "resolve_tracking_uri" src/minivess/ --include="*.py"
# The function should already handle HTTPS URLs, but verify it doesn't # reject dagshub.com specifically. If it does, update the validation.
task.verification: resolve_tracking_uri() returns the DagsHub URL when MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI is set to https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow. No hardcoded URL patterns that would reject DagsHub.

phase.name: RunPod SkyPilot YAML Updates
phase.description: Update SkyPilot YAML files to use DagsHub MLflow instead of file-based or GCP Cloud Run MLflow. Create a RunPod-primary train_factorial.yaml variant. NOTE: CLAUDE.md Rule #31 — YAML configs are SPECIFICATIONS. Only change what the user has explicitly authorized (MLflow URI, DVC remote, cloud target).

task.name: Update smoke_test_gpu.yaml for DagsHub MLflow
task.description: Update the RunPod smoke test YAML to log to DagsHub MLflow instead of file-based /opt/vol/mlruns. This is the first cloud integration test.
task.changes:
# In deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml, UPDATE the envs section:
envs: # ... (MODEL_FAMILY, etc. unchanged)
# MLflow: DagsHub hosted server (replaces file-based /opt/vol/mlruns) # Auth: MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME + MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD from --env-file .env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME} MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD}
# MLflow client resilience — retry on transient network failures MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_MAX_RETRIES: "10" MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT: "300"
task.verification: grep "dagshub.com" deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml returns the MLflow URI. MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME and MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD are present in envs.
task.notes: The --env-file .env flag in the launch command passes MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME and MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD from the local .env to the SkyPilot job. SkyPilot resolves ${VAR} from --env-file, not from intra-envs references.

task.name: Create train_factorial_runpod.yaml
task.description: Create a RunPod-targeted variant of train_factorial.yaml. This replaces the GCP-specific YAML for the factorial experiment. Uses DagsHub MLflow and DagsHub DVC (with Network Volume cache fallback for data).
task.changes:
# Create: deployment/skypilot/train_factorial_runpod.yaml # Based on train_factorial.yaml but targeting RunPod + DagsHub. # Key differences from GCP version: # - cloud: runpod (not gcp) # - image_id: docker:petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest (Docker Hub, not GAR) # - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: DagsHub (not Cloud Run) # - DVC: DagsHub remote (not GCS) # - Network Volume for weight caching # - No spot recovery (RunPod community cloud doesn't support it well)
# resources: # image_id: docker:petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest # accelerators: {RTX4090: 1, RTX5090: 1, RTX3090: 1} # cloud: runpod # use_spot: false # On-demand for reliability on community cloud # disk_size: 60
# volumes: # /opt/vol: minivess-dev
# envs: # MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow # MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME} # MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD} # ... (all factorial env vars same as train_factorial.yaml)
# setup: # 1. HuggingFace login (same as current) # 2. Check Network Volume for data (fast path) # 3. If no data on volume: DVC pull from DagsHub remote # 4. Weight caching on Network Volume (HF_HOME, TORCH_HOME) # 5. Pre-flight checks (GPU, MLflow connectivity, data)
# run: (identical to train_factorial.yaml)
task.verification: sky.Task.from_yaml('deployment/skypilot/train_factorial_runpod.yaml') parses OK. All env vars from train_factorial.yaml are present.
task.notes: The GCP train_factorial.yaml is PRESERVED for labs that want GCP. The RunPod version is the NEW DEFAULT for the project owner. run_factorial.sh should be updated to select the correct YAML based on SKYPILOT_DEFAULT_CLOUD or a --cloud flag.

task.name: Update setup block for DagsHub DVC + Network Volume data
task.description: The setup block in the RunPod factorial YAML needs a dual-path data strategy: 1. Check Network Volume first (fast path, zero network cost) 2. If data not present, DVC pull from DagsHub remote This replaces the GCS DVC pull in the GCP version.
task.changes:
# In the setup: block of train_factorial_runpod.yaml:
setup: | set -ex cd /app
# HuggingFace login (same as current, with retry) # ... (unchanged from train_factorial.yaml)
# Data: Network Volume cache-first, DagsHub DVC fallback. # Network Volume path: /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ # DagsHub DVC path: dvc pull -r dagshub DATA_DIR=/opt/vol/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr if [ -d "${DATA_DIR}" ] && [ "$(ls "${DATA_DIR}" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)" -gt 10 ]; then echo "Training data found on Network Volume ($(ls ${DATA_DIR} | wc -l) files)" mkdir -p data/raw ln -sf /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess data/raw/minivess else echo "Data not on Network Volume — pulling from DagsHub DVC..." dvc init --no-scm -f 2>/dev/null || true dvc remote add -f dagshub s3://dvc dvc remote modify dagshub endpointurl https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3 # Auth: use DAGSHUB_TOKEN env var (passed via --env-file .env) dvc remote modify dagshub --local access_key_id "${DAGSHUB_TOKEN}" dvc remote modify dagshub --local secret_access_key "${DAGSHUB_TOKEN}"
dvc_pull_ok=false for attempt in 1 2 3; do if timeout 600 dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r dagshub; then dvc_pull_ok=true break else echo "DVC pull attempt ${attempt} failed" && sleep $((30 * attempt)) fi done if [ "${dvc_pull_ok}" = "false" ]; then echo "FATAL: DVC pull from DagsHub failed after 3 attempts." exit 33 fi fi
# Weight caching: Network Volume (download once, reuse forever) export HF_HOME=/opt/vol/.cache/huggingface export TORCH_HOME=/opt/vol/.cache/torch mkdir -p $HF_HOME $TORCH_HOME
# Pre-cache model weights (same logic as current, with retry) # ... (unchanged from train_factorial.yaml)
# MLflow connectivity check (UPDATED for DagsHub) echo "Checking DagsHub MLflow connectivity..." timeout 30 python -c " import os, sys uri = os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI'] from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=uri) exps = client.search_experiments(max_results=1) print(f'MLflow OK: {uri} ({len(exps)} experiments)') " || { echo "FATAL: DagsHub MLflow server is not reachable." echo "Check: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME, MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD" exit 1 }
task.verification: Setup block handles both paths: Network Volume data (fast) and DagsHub DVC pull (fallback). MLflow connectivity check passes against DagsHub endpoint.

task.name: Test smoke test on RunPod with DagsHub MLflow
task.description: Launch the updated smoke test YAML on RunPod to verify end-to-end: Docker image pull -> DagsHub MLflow logging -> Training -> Results on DagsHub.
task.commands:
# Launch smoke test (DynUNet, 2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes) sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env-file .env \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ -y
# Monitor job sky jobs logs -f
# After completion, verify on DagsHub: # 1. Visit https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia/experiments # 2. Look for the smoke test run with metrics (loss, dice, etc.) # 3. Verify params logged correctly (model_family=dynunet, etc.)
task.verification: Job completes with exit code 0. MLflow run visible on DagsHub experiment tracking UI. Metrics (loss, dice) and params (model_family, etc.) logged correctly. No "mlruns is file-based" warnings — everything goes to DagsHub.
task.rollback: If DagsHub MLflow fails, fall back to file-based: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/opt/vol/mlruns (the previous working configuration).

phase.name: DVC Data Migration
phase.description: Push DVC-tracked data to DagsHub storage. This makes data available for DVC pull from any machine (RunPod, collaborator laptops, CI). Currently data is on local filesystem and GCS (now torn down).

task.name: Push MiniVess data to DagsHub
task.description: Push the MiniVess training dataset (~1.5 GB) to DagsHub DVC storage. The data is already DVC-tracked locally.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
# Verify DVC remote is configured dvc remote list dvc remote default
# Check what data is tracked dvc status
# Push MiniVess data to DagsHub dvc push data/raw/minivess -r dagshub
# Verify push (file count and sizes) echo "Local file count:" find data/raw/minivess -type f | wc -l echo "DVC cache status:" dvc status -c -r dagshub
task.verification: dvc push completes without errors. dvc status -c -r dagshub shows all files are up to date.

task.name: Push DeepVess data to DagsHub
task.description: Push the DeepVess external test dataset (~0.5 GB) to DagsHub storage. Used for zero-shot evaluation and generalization testing.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
# Push DeepVess data dvc push data/raw/deepvess -r dagshub 2>/dev/null || \ dvc push data/raw/deepvess.dvc -r dagshub
# Verify echo "DeepVess files:" find data/raw/deepvess -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l || echo "No deepvess dir"
task.verification: DeepVess data push completes. dvc status shows up to date.

task.name: Verify data integrity on DagsHub
task.description: Verify that data pushed to DagsHub can be pulled back correctly. Test from a clean DVC state to simulate a new collaborator.
task.commands:
cd /tmp mkdir -p dagshub-dvc-test && cd dagshub-dvc-test
# Clone repo (or init minimal DVC project) git clone https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.git test-pull cd test-pull
# Configure DVC remote dvc remote add dagshub s3://dvc dvc remote modify dagshub endpointurl https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3 dvc remote modify dagshub --local access_key_id "$DAGSHUB_TOKEN" dvc remote modify dagshub --local secret_access_key "$DAGSHUB_TOKEN"
# Pull data dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r dagshub
# Verify file counts match echo "Pulled file count:" find data/raw/minivess -type f | wc -l
# Cleanup cd /tmp && rm -rf dagshub-dvc-test
task.verification: File count matches local repo. No corruption or missing files. A fresh clone + dvc pull reproduces the exact dataset.

task.name: Update .dvc/config to reflect new remote hierarchy
task.description: Update .dvc/config to show dagshub as default, minio for local, and GCS as archived/optional. Remove any stale remote references.
task.changes: # .dvc/config should look like: [core] remote = dagshub ['remote "dagshub"'] url = s3://dvc endpointurl = https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3 ['remote "minio"'] url = s3://dvc-data endpointurl = http://localhost:9000 access_key_id = minioadmin secret_access_key = minioadmin # GCS remote preserved for labs that use GCP (OPTIONAL) ['remote "gcs"'] url = gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data
task.verification: dvc remote default returns "dagshub". dvc remote list shows dagshub, minio, gcs.

phase.name: Knowledge Graph and Config Updates
phase.description: Update all configuration files, knowledge graph, and documentation to reflect the new architecture: RunPod primary + DagsHub for tracking/data. GCP preserved as optional/documented alternative.

task.name: Update knowledge-graph/domains/cloud.yaml
task.description: Update the cloud domain YAML to reflect: RunPod as primary compute for ALL environments, DagsHub as primary MLflow + DVC backend, GCP as archived/optional.
task.changes:
# Key updates to cloud.yaml:
# 1. cloud_provider_selection: update winner decisions: cloud_provider_selection: status: resolved winner: runpod_dagshub rationale: > TWO providers: RunPod (primary compute, all environments) + DagsHub (MLflow tracking + DVC storage, free for public repos). GCP fully torn down 2026-03-28 after 26+ hour GPU drought. GCP preserved as documented optional path for well-funded labs. implementation: [configs/cloud/runpod_dev.yaml, .env.example]
# 2. dvc_remote_strategy: update to dagshub dvc_remote_strategy: status: resolved winner: dagshub_primary_minio_local rationale: > DagsHub S3-compatible storage for cloud DVC remote (free, 20 GB). MinIO for local Docker Compose dev (unchanged). GCS preserved as optional for labs with GCP infrastructure. implementation: [.dvc/config]
# 3. Add dagshub provider entry providers: dagshub: environment: "all" role: "MLflow tracking server + DVC storage + Git mirror" status: active mlflow_uri: "https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow" dvc_endpoint: "https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3" storage_limit: "20 GB (free individual tier, unlimited experiments for public)" pricing: "$0/month (free for public repos)"
# 4. Update RunPod provider runpod: environment: "all (primary compute for debug + staging + production)" role: "Primary GPU compute for all experiment passes" status: active mlflow: "DagsHub hosted (https://dagshub.com/.../vascadia.mlflow)"
# 5. Archive GCP gcp: environment: "archived-optional" role: "Optional for well-funded labs. Fully torn down 2026-03-28." status: archived

task.name: Update CLAUDE.md two-provider architecture
task.description: Update the two-provider architecture table in CLAUDE.md. RunPod becomes primary compute (all environments). DagsHub becomes the tracking/data backend. GCP becomes archived/optional.
task.changes:
# Update the Two-Provider Cloud Architecture table:
| Provider | Environment | Role | Data Storage | MLflow | |----------|------------|------|--------------|--------| | **RunPod** | all (primary) | Primary compute — RTX 4090 for all experiment passes | Network Volume (weight cache) | DagsHub hosted | | **DagsHub** | all (tracking) | MLflow tracking server + DVC storage (free, public) | DagsHub Storage (20 GB free) | https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow | | ~~GCP~~ | archived | Optional for well-funded labs — torn down 2026-03-28 | GCS (if re-enabled) | Cloud Run (if re-enabled) |
# Update the Three-Environment Model: | Environment | Docker | Compute | Data | MLflow | Purpose | |-------------|--------|---------|------|--------|---------| | **local** | Docker Compose | Local GPU | MinIO (local) | DagsHub or mlruns/ | Fast iteration | | **dev/staging** | Docker via SkyPilot | RunPod RTX 4090 | Network Volume + DagsHub DVC | DagsHub | Experiments | | **production** | Docker via SkyPilot | RunPod RTX 4090 | Network Volume + DagsHub DVC | DagsHub | Paper results |
task.notes: CLAUDE.md is the constitution (L0). Changes here propagate to all sessions. The two-provider model changes from RunPod+GCP to RunPod+DagsHub. GCP references should be kept but marked as archived/optional.

task.name: Update deployment/CLAUDE.md
task.description: Update deployment CLAUDE.md with DagsHub MLflow configuration details. Update Docker image registry references (Docker Hub primary, GAR archived).
task.changes:
# Add DagsHub section to deployment/CLAUDE.md:
## DagsHub Integration (Primary Tracking Backend)
MLflow tracking: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow DVC storage: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3 Free tier: 20 GB storage, unlimited experiments (public repo).
Auth env vars (in .env): MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME=petteriTeikari MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD=<DAGSHUB_TOKEN>
# Update Docker registry section: Docker Hub is the PRIMARY registry (RunPod pulls without auth). GAR is ARCHIVED (GCP torn down 2026-03-28).

task.name: Update configs/cloud/runpod_dev.yaml
task.description: Update the RunPod cloud config to reflect DagsHub MLflow (not file-based). This config is referenced by SkyPilot YAML files and run_factorial.sh.
task.changes:
# In configs/cloud/runpod_dev.yaml, UPDATE:
# MLflow: DagsHub hosted (replaces file-based on Network Volume) mlflow_tracking_uri: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow mlflow_env_var: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI
# DVC remote: DagsHub (replaces GCS/file-based) dvc_remote: dagshub

task.name: Update preflight checks for RunPod + DagsHub
task.description: Update scripts/preflight_gcp.py (or create scripts/preflight_runpod.py) to check DagsHub connectivity instead of GCP infrastructure.
task.changes: # The preflight script should check: # 1. RunPod API key configured (sky check runpod) # 2. Network Volume exists (sky storage ls | grep minivess-dev) # 3. DagsHub MLflow reachable (curl -s https://dagshub.com/.../vascadia.mlflow/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/list) # 4. DagsHub DVC reachable (dvc status -r dagshub) # 5. Docker image exists on Docker Hub (docker manifest inspect petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest) # 6. .env has required variables (DAGSHUB_TOKEN, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, HF_TOKEN)

task.name: Update run_factorial.sh for RunPod + DagsHub
task.description: Update the factorial launch script to use the RunPod YAML by default and pass DagsHub credentials via --env-file.
task.changes: # In scripts/run_factorial.sh: # 1. Default YAML: deployment/skypilot/train_factorial_runpod.yaml (was train_factorial.yaml) # 2. Add --cloud runpod flag support # 3. Pass --env-file .env for DagsHub credentials # 4. Update the --env overrides to include MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME/PASSWORD # 5. Update parallel submission config from configs/cloud/runpod_dev.yaml

phase.name: Verification and Launch
phase.description: End-to-end verification of the new architecture. Run tests locally, then launch a validation job on RunPod with DagsHub MLflow.

task.name: Run make test-staging
task.description: Verify all local tests pass with the new configuration. This catches any broken imports, config references, or test assumptions.
task.verification: All tests pass. Zero failures, zero unexpected skips.

task.name: Run preflight checks
task.description: Run the updated preflight script to verify all infrastructure is ready.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
# Manual preflight checks (until script is updated) echo "=== Preflight Check: RunPod + DagsHub ==="
# 1. RunPod API key sky check runpod 2>&1 | tail -3
# 2. Network Volume sky storage ls 2>&1 | grep minivess-dev || echo "WARNING: Network Volume not found"
# 3. DagsHub MLflow curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \ -u "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME}:${MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD}" \ "https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/list" echo " (200 = OK)"
# 4. Docker image docker manifest inspect petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest > /dev/null 2>&1 \ && echo "Docker image: OK" || echo "Docker image: NOT FOUND"
# 5. .env variables for var in DAGSHUB_TOKEN MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME HF_TOKEN; do if grep -q "^${var}=.\+" .env 2>/dev/null; then echo "${var}: SET" else echo "${var}: MISSING" fi done
task.verification: All 5 checks pass: RunPod OK, Network Volume found, DagsHub MLflow returns 200, Docker image exists, all env vars set.

task.name: Launch Phase 1 validation job (DynUNet on RTX 4090)
task.description: Launch a single DynUNet training job on RunPod RTX 4090 with DagsHub MLflow. This is the simplest model — validates the full pipeline without SAM3 weight download complexity.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
# Launch single DynUNet smoke test sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env-file .env \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ -y
# Monitor sky jobs logs -f
# After completion: echo "=== Verification ===" echo "1. Check DagsHub: https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia/experiments" echo "2. Verify metrics: loss, dice, hausdorff logged" echo "3. Verify params: model_family=dynunet, epochs=2, etc."
task.verification: Job completes with exit code 0. DagsHub shows the experiment run with all expected metrics and params.

task.name: Verify MLflow run appears on DagsHub
task.description: Programmatically verify the MLflow run from T5.3 is visible on DagsHub.
task.commands:
cd /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia
uv run python -c " import dagshub import mlflow
dagshub.init(repo_owner='petteriTeikari', repo_name='vascadia')
client = mlflow.MlflowClient()
# List all experiments experiments = client.search_experiments() print(f'Total experiments: {len(experiments)}') for exp in experiments: print(f' {exp.name} (id={exp.experiment_id})')
# Find the most recent run runs = client.search_runs( experiment_ids=[exp.experiment_id for exp in experiments], max_results=5, order_by=['start_time DESC'] ) print(f'\nMost recent runs:') for run in runs: metrics = {k: f'{v:.4f}' for k, v in run.data.metrics.items()} print(f' {run.info.run_name}: {metrics}') print(f' params: {run.data.params}') "
task.verification: The DynUNet smoke test run is visible with metrics and params. At least one run has model_family=dynunet in params.

task.name: Verify DVC data accessible from RunPod
task.description: Verify that a RunPod job can DVC pull data from DagsHub storage. This tests the fallback path (when data is NOT on Network Volume).
task.commands:
# This is tested implicitly by T5.3 if Network Volume already has data. # To test the DVC pull path explicitly, temporarily rename the data dir:
# SSH into RunPod pod (if still running): # sky exec minivess-smoke-test -- "mv /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess_backup"
# Then re-run with DVC pull path: # sky exec minivess-smoke-test -- "cd /app && dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r dagshub"
# Or test locally: cd /tmp && mkdir dvc-pull-test && cd dvc-pull-test git clone https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.git . dvc remote add dagshub s3://dvc dvc remote modify dagshub endpointurl https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.s3 dvc remote modify dagshub --local access_key_id "$DAGSHUB_TOKEN" dvc remote modify dagshub --local secret_access_key "$DAGSHUB_TOKEN" dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r dagshub ls data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ | wc -l cd /tmp && rm -rf dvc-pull-test
task.verification: DVC pull from DagsHub succeeds. File count matches expected (~47 training volumes).

appendix.name: Multi-Hypothesis Decision Matrix
appendix.description: Open-ended evaluation of the user's decisions against alternative hypotheses. All decisions have been MADE by the user — this appendix provides the evidence base and documents the reasoning for future reference.

hypothesis.name: DagsHub Hosted MLflow
hypothesis.description: MLflow tracking at https://dagshub.com/petteriTeikari/vascadia.mlflow. Free for public repos, unlimited experiments, 20 GB artifact storage.
hypothesis.pros: - Zero cost for public repos - Zero infrastructure management - Web UI for team/reviewer experiment browsing - MLflow 3.x compatible (confirmed by DagsHub blog post) - Standard MLflow API — zero code changes to tracking calls - DVC integration in same platform - Open science: public MLflow UI for paper readers
hypothesis.cons: - 20 GB artifact storage limit (tight for SAM3 checkpoints ~900 MB each) - Vendor dependency (DagsHub is a smaller company) - Internet required (no offline training with tracking) - Latency: network round-trip for every mlflow.log_metric() call - 100 experiment limit in private repos (irrelevant — repo is public)
hypothesis.mitigations: - Artifact size: Log metrics/params only, keep checkpoints on Network Volume - Vendor risk: All data exportable via standard MLflow API + DVC commands - Offline: File-based fallback (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=mlruns) always works - Latency: MLflow batches writes; minimal impact on training speed

hypothesis.name: File-Based MLflow (Ephemeral Sync)
hypothesis.description: MLflow writes to /opt/vol/mlruns on Network Volume. Post-job: sky rsync down to local mlruns/.
hypothesis.pros: - Zero external dependencies - Zero latency (local filesystem writes) - Works offline - Maximum reproducibility (no service dependency)
hypothesis.cons: - No web UI for sharing experiments - Must remember sky rsync down after every job - Local-only access (no collaboration) - No public experiment URLs for paper
hypothesis.verdict: Better for pure reproducibility, worse for open science narrative

hypothesis.name: W&B Academic Plan
hypothesis.description: Weights & Biases with free Academic plan (200 GB, 100 seats).
hypothesis.pros: - 200 GB storage (10x DagsHub) - 100 team seats - Best-in-class experiment visualization - Largest ML community
hypothesis.cons: - Requires .edu email (may not have one) - NOT MLflow-native — requires code migration to wandb.log() - Proprietary API (vendor lock-in) - Closed-source server
hypothesis.verdict: Rejected: requires MLflow code migration, proprietary API

hypothesis.name: Self-Hosted MLflow on VPS
hypothesis.description: MLflow server on Hetzner CX22 (EUR 3.79/month) or similar VPS.
hypothesis.pros: - Full control - Zero vendor lock-in - Unlimited storage (VPS disk)
hypothesis.cons: - $4-20/month ongoing cost - Server maintenance burden - Must handle TLS, auth, backups - Reviewer must also set up or use your server
hypothesis.verdict: Rejected: unnecessary complexity for solo/small-team project

decision.name: DVC Storage Backend
decision.user_choice: DagsHub Storage (with Connect GCS for overflow)

hypothesis.name: DagsHub Storage Primary
hypothesis.description: S3-compatible DVC remote at https://dagshub.com/USER/REPO.s3. 20 GB free, standard DVC push/pull commands.
hypothesis.pros: - Zero cost (free tier) - Zero setup (auto-configured with repo) - Standard DVC commands (no code changes) - Same platform as MLflow (unified tooling) - Public data access for collaborators
hypothesis.cons: - 20 GB limit (~2 GB used now, headroom for growth) - Upload speed depends on DagsHub servers - Cannot host very large datasets (>20 GB need overflow)
hypothesis.storage_estimate: MiniVess: ~1.5 GB DeepVess: ~0.5 GB VesselNN (drift): ~0.3 GB Headroom: ~17.7 GB free
hypothesis.overflow_plan: If 20 GB is reached: Connect external GCS bucket via DagsHub Connect External Storage feature. GCS Standard Storage: $0.02/GB/month. 10 GB overflow = $0.20/month.

hypothesis.name: GCS Direct (Current, Archived)
hypothesis.pros: - Unlimited storage ($0.02/GB/month) - Enterprise-grade reliability - Same-region as GCP compute (zero egress when on GCP)
hypothesis.cons: - Requires GCP project + billing + IAM - GCP fully torn down (would need re-provisioning) - Egress fees from GCP to RunPod ($0.12/GB) - Not accessible without GCP account
hypothesis.verdict: Archived: GCP infrastructure torn down, costly to maintain

hypothesis.name: Network Volume Only (No DVC Remote)
hypothesis.description: Data lives only on RunPod Network Volume and local filesystem. No cloud DVC remote — rsync for transfer.
hypothesis.pros: - Simplest possible setup - Zero cloud storage cost - Fastest data access (local NVMe)
hypothesis.cons: - No DVC pull for collaborators - Single point of failure (volume loss = re-upload from local) - Not reproducible without the specific Network Volume
hypothesis.verdict: Too fragile for an open-source academic project

hypothesis.name: RunPod RTX 4090
hypothesis.description: Community Cloud on-demand at $0.34/hr, 24 GB VRAM, 82.6 BF16 TFLOPS, 1008 GB/s memory bandwidth.
hypothesis.evidence: - 11 experiment passes of empirical data - Instant provisioning (vs 26h GCP drought) - Cost-efficiency champion for this workload - Consumer GPU narrative for Nature Protocols - All models fit in 24 GB (DynUNet ~2 GB, SAM3 Hybrid ~7.5 GB, VesselFM ~10 GB)

hypothesis.name: RunPod RTX 5090
hypothesis.verdict: Higher throughput but 70% higher hourly cost for 50% speedup. Not cost-efficient.

hypothesis.name: GCP L4 Spot
hypothesis.description: $0.22/hr spot, but 26+ hours of zero availability in 3 regions.
hypothesis.verdict: Cheapest per-GPU-hour but unreliable and heavy infra overhead.

hypothesis.name: RunPod A100
hypothesis.verdict: Overkill for single-GPU 3D segmentation. 4090 is sufficient.

decision.name: DagsHub Repository Visibility
decision.rationale: Public repo on DagsHub = unlimited experiments + unlimited tracked metrics. Private repos limited to 100 experiments and 2 collaborators. Open science alignment: Nature Protocols paper = open data, open code, open results. Public MLflow UI means reviewers can browse experiments in browser. Zero downside for an academic project intended for publication.

decision.name: Artifact Storage Strategy
decision.user_choice: User decided: DagsHub for tracking, Network Volume for large artifacts

hypothesis.name: Metrics to DagsHub, Checkpoints to Network Volume
hypothesis.description: Log metrics, params, tags, small plots to DagsHub MLflow. Keep model checkpoints (~900 MB per SAM3 run) on Network Volume. Checkpoints synced to local via sky rsync down (existing pattern).
hypothesis.pros: - 20 GB DagsHub storage lasts for hundreds of experiment runs - Network Volume provides fast local access to checkpoints - No artifact upload bottleneck during training - Separation of concerns: tracking (DagsHub) vs artifacts (local)
hypothesis.cons: - Checkpoints not browsable on DagsHub web UI - Must sky rsync down to get checkpoints locally - Two storage locations instead of one

hypothesis.name: Everything to DagsHub MLflow
hypothesis.description: Log ALL artifacts (including checkpoints) to DagsHub.
hypothesis.cons: - 20 GB exhausted in ~20 SAM3 runs (900 MB checkpoints each) - Slow artifact uploads over internet during training - Would need paid tier ($99/month) within weeks
hypothesis.verdict: Rejected: storage limit too tight for checkpoint logging

tier.name: Team
tier.storage: Up to 1 TB or 2 million files

section.name: MLflow Integration
section.auth_env_vars: MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME: DagsHub username MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD: DagsHub token
section.python_init: import dagshub dagshub.init(repo_owner="petteriTeikari", repo_name="vascadia") # Auto-configures: mlflow.set_tracking_uri() + auth

section.name: DVC Remote
section.auth: access_key_id: DagsHub token secret_access_key: DagsHub token (same value for both)

section.name: GitHub Mirror
section.method: Web UI only (no programmatic API for initial connection)
section.features: - PRs sync bidirectionally (GitHub to DagsHub and vice versa) - Issues sync bidirectionally - Git-tracked files stay on GitHub; DagsHub adds DVC storage + MLflow

section.name: Connect External Storage (GCS Overflow)
section.description: If 20 GB DagsHub storage is exceeded, connect an external GCS bucket. DagsHub supports AWS S3, GCS, Azure Blob, and S3-compatible storage.
section.method: Web UI (Settings -> Storage -> Connect External Storage)

risk.name: RunPod RTX 4090 shortage
risk.mitigation: RTX 3090 ($0.40/hr) or RTX 5090 ($0.58/hr) as fallback. SkyPilot accelerators list already includes all three.

risk.name: RunPod Network Volume data loss
risk.mitigation: All data is DVC-versioned on DagsHub + local filesystem. Re-upload from local in ~30 min. Network Volume is on NVMe SSD with RunPod's infrastructure reliability.

risk.name: DagsHub discontinues free tier or reduces limits
risk.mitigation: File-based MLflow (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=mlruns) is the zero-dependency fallback. All experiment data exportable via standard MLflow API. DVC data portable to any S3-compatible storage.

risk.name: SkyPilot-RunPod integration breaks after upgrade
risk.mitigation: Pin SkyPilot version in pyproject.toml. Test before each factorial pass with smoke test. SkyPilot has active maintainers and RunPod integration is officially supported.

risk.name: RunPod community host goes offline mid-job
risk.mitigation: SkyPilot auto-retries. Network Volume persists data across pod restarts. MLflow metrics already logged to DagsHub (not lost with pod). Use on-demand (not spot) for critical production runs.

risk.name: DagsHub MLflow latency affects training speed
risk.mitigation: MLflow client batches writes. Empirically, MLflow tracking overhead is less than 1% of training time. If problematic, switch to async logging or file-based fallback for the training loop, sync to DagsHub post-run.

risk.name: 20 GB DagsHub storage exhausted
risk.mitigation: Current usage ~2 GB (MiniVess + DeepVess). Growth rate ~50 MB per debug pass (metrics only, no checkpoints). Would take 360+ debug passes to exhaust. If needed: connect external GCS bucket ($0.02/GB/month) or upgrade to Team tier ($99/month).

checklist.item: Connect GitHub mirror via web UI (MANUAL)
Update setup block for DagsHub DVC
Push MiniVess data to DagsHub DVC
Push DeepVess data to DagsHub DVC

file.reason: Add DagsHub vars, update MLflow URI, update default cloud
Add dagshub remote, set as default

appendix.name: Sources
appendix.source: DagsHub Pricing — Individual tier: 20 GB, unlimited public experiments
DagsHub MLflow Integration — URI format, auth, dagshub.init()
DagsHub DVC Remote — S3-compatible endpoint, auth config
DagsHub GitHub Integration — webhook sync, PR/issue mirroring
DagsHub Git Mirror — 24h auto-sync, manual trigger
DagsHub Python Client — dagshub.init(), auth, upload
DagsHub Connect External Storage — GCS, S3, Azure overflow
SkyPilot Docker Containers — image_id: docker: usage
SkyPilot Volumes — RunPod Network Volume support
